title: Weekly Surge In Google Trends Searches For Emergency Hvac Repair Signals Consumer Stress Lifting Discretionary Spending
thesis: A rising number of emergency HVAC repair searches often correlates with increased discretionary spending on home comfort upgrades. Consumer discretionary spending rises as consumers address urgent home comfort needs.
signal_logic: If weekly Google Trends index for 'emergency HVAC repair' rises more than 15% week-over-week
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly Google Trends index for 'emergency HVAC repair' rises more than 15% week-over-week Exit: After 4 weeks or when searches decline 10% week-over-week
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly searches via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: HVAC system failures and weather variability cause frequent spikes in emergency repair searches.

schema.idea: Weekly surge in Google Trends searches for 'emergency HVAC repair' signals consumer stress lifting discretionary spending
schema.behavior: A rising number of emergency HVAC repair searches often correlates with increased discretionary spending on home comfort upgrades.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer discretionary spending rises as consumers address urgent home comfort needs.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Google Trends index for 'emergency HVAC repair' rises more than 15% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or when searches decline 10% week-over-week

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: HVAC system failures and weather variability cause frequent spikes in emergency repair searches.

title: Daily Spike In Google Trends Searches For Gas Station Price Hike Signals Consumer Stress And Energy Sector Pressure
thesis: Rapid increases in searches for gas price hikes indicate consumer stress from fuel costs, pressuring energy demand sentiment. Consumer stress from rising gas prices can reduce discretionary fuel consumption and increase inflation concerns.
signal_logic: If daily Google Trends index for 'gas station price hike' rises 20% day-over-day
entry_exit: Entry: If daily Google Trends index for 'gas station price hike' rises 20% day-over-day Exit: After 7 days or when index falls below 10% above baseline
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends daily search interest via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Fuel price volatility and consumer attention cause frequent short-term search spikes.

schema.idea: Daily spike in Google Trends searches for 'gas station price hike' signals consumer stress and energy sector pressure
schema.behavior: Rapid increases in searches for gas price hikes indicate consumer stress from fuel costs, pressuring energy demand sentiment.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer stress from rising gas prices can reduce discretionary fuel consumption and increase inflation concerns.

trade_logic.entry: If daily Google Trends index for 'gas station price hike' rises 20% day-over-day
trade_logic.exit: After 7 days or when index falls below 10% above baseline

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Fuel price volatility and consumer attention cause frequent short-term search spikes.
